coreIssue: This performance conversation is likely about whether Tom is operating with Principal-level scope on the new team or staying in strong but narrower execution mode.
openingQuestion: What decisions, alignment moves, or cross-team outcomes do you see as your Principal-level contribution on this team right now?
listenFor: whether he names a specific recent decision, stakeholder conversation, or tradeoff he shaped beyond his own delivery
whether he pauses or stays abstract when asked what Principal scope means on this team versus describing concrete expectations
if they mention waiting for direction, late alignment, or avoiding hard calls until others weigh in
avoid: do not frame steady delivery as sufficient evidence of Principal performance in a feedback meeting
do not jump straight to telling him the right Principal model before testing his current assumptions
goodOutcome: You and Tom have agreed on one concrete marker of Principal-level scope on this team, such as an end-to-end cross-team decision area he should visibly own.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example where Tom delivered well but Principal-level leverage or early alignment was missing so your feedback stays specific.
